title: Investment Comparison
context: Option A: Savings account, 4% AER, instant access. Option B: Fixed bond, 5.5% for 2 years (no withdrawals). Option C: Stocks & Shares ISA, average 7% but can go up or down.

questions.question: What does 'fixed bond' mean?
options: Your money is locked away for a set period
questions.answer: Your money is locked away for a set period

questions.question: What is 'risk tolerance'?
options: How much financial risk you are comfortable with
questions.answer: How much financial risk you are comfortable with

maths_section.instruction: Compare £10,000 invested in each option for 2 years.

questions.question: Option B earns how much more than A?
Option C could return 7% or lose 10%. Best case vs worst case after 1 year?
